mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and housing starts indicates a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes corporate profit margins, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict negative SPX returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, rising real savings deposits reflect precautionary household behavior that drains equity market liquidity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns show no significant negative correlation with RSAFS growth when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or the signal predictive power vanishes during quantitative easing phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real GDP expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, generating positive forward equity returns over a six-month horizon regardless of short-term volatility.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth fails to positively correlate with 60-day SPX returns in more than 40% of observations, or rolling 3-year Rank IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production index falling below 100.0 indicates manufacturing capacity utilization stress that suppresses corporate revenue growth, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where INDPRO drops below 100.0, or the signal exhibits no statistical edge over a random baseline in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth combined with rising unemployment reflects a labor market cooling cycle where job creation slows faster than layoffs, signaling impending earnings revisions that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over a week.
failure_condition: The combined signal of PAYEMS deceleration and UNRATE acceleration fails to predict negative SPX returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, rising consumer credit balances indicate debt-fueled consumption that eventually triggers monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not decline when RSAFS expands alongside CPIAUCSL above 240.0, or the predictive relationship reverses sign during disinflationary periods.
